title: Pancreatitis Hospitalizes More Americans Than Any Other GI Disease. Treatment Is Finally Catching Up.
description: Pancreatitis hospitalizes more Americans than any other gastrointestinal disease, its global case count has risen 59% since 1990, and it quietly opens a door to pancreatic cancer that most patients never know exists. Now, after decades of medicine offering little beyond IV fluids and waiting, a landmark RNA-targeted drug has cut pancreatitis risk by 85% in high-risk patients — and the old dogma of starving the pancreas to rest it has been overturned entirely. This is the story of a disease medicine underestimated, and what it's finally learning to do about it.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for The Pancreas Has Been Quietly Destroying Itself. Medicine Is Only Now Learning to Stop It.
